metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Drama 11 deepens students' performance skills, theatrical knowledge, and creative capacities developed in Drama 10. Students refine their acting technique, explore a wider range of theatrical styles and forms, devise and perform original works, and engage in critical analysis of theatre as an art form with social and cultural power. First Peoples performance traditions and diverse global theatrical forms are central. Drama 11 prepares students for Drama 12, Theatre Company, Theatre Production, Directing, and Film and Television pathways.
metadata.general_description: An advanced senior drama course developing refined acting technique, theatrical analysis, devised performance, script interpretation, and the social and cultural dimensions of theatre — preparing students for Drama 12 and specialized theatre pathways.

big_ideas.statement: Drama communicates complex ideas, emotions, and cultural perspectives through integrated theatrical elements.
big_ideas.elaboration: Advanced theatrical practice integrates acting, direction, design, and dramaturgy — each element contributing to a layered, multi-dimensional communication of meaning and experience.

big_ideas.statement: Theatre illuminates the human condition and fosters empathy across cultures and time.
big_ideas.elaboration: Through inhabiting diverse characters and situations, drama practitioners develop deep empathy, cultural understanding, and insight into perspectives different from their own.

big_ideas.statement: Theatre is both a reflection of and a force for social and cultural change.
big_ideas.elaboration: From Greek tragedy to contemporary verbatim theatre, drama has always engaged with the pressing questions of its time — challenging power, amplifying marginalized voices, and imagining alternative futures.

big_ideas.statement: Artistic growth requires ongoing commitment, vulnerability, critical self-reflection, and creative risk.
big_ideas.elaboration: Advanced dramatic work demands sustained engagement with one's craft — facing technical and emotional challenges, learning from mistakes, and developing the artistic courage to create and perform authentically.

competencies.raw: Apply refined acting techniques and conventions to create complex characters; devise original theatrical works; explore diverse theatrical forms and styles; take purposeful creative and artistic risks.
i_can_statements: I can apply refined acting techniques — Stanislavski, Brecht, physical theatre — to create complex, believable characters.
I can devise original theatrical works collaboratively from a variety of stimuli and social issues.
I can explore a range of theatrical forms — realism, absurdism, musical theatre, physical theatre — with growing fluency.
I can take purposeful creative risks in performance and devising, driven by clear artistic intent.

competencies.raw: Analyze and evaluate theatrical works using advanced drama vocabulary; apply detailed feedback to refine artistic practice; examine theatre in social, cultural, historical, and political contexts.
i_can_statements: I can analyze and evaluate theatrical works — including my own — using advanced dramatic vocabulary.
I can apply detailed feedback to refine my acting, directing, and devising practice.
I can examine theatre in its social, cultural, historical, and political contexts — understanding what theatre does in the world.

competencies.raw: Perform in varied theatrical contexts with technical skill and artistic commitment; document the artistic process and growth; use theatre to address social and community issues.
i_can_statements: I can perform in varied theatrical contexts — class studio, stage, community — with consistent technical skill and expressive commitment.
I can document my artistic development through rehearsal journals, video analysis, and reflective essays.
I can use theatre to give voice to social, cultural, and community issues in ways that invite meaningful audience response.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate sustained professionalism in rehearsal and performance; engage with First Peoples theatrical traditions; examine theatre as a tool for cultural transmission and social change.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate sustained professionalism — preparation, reliability, and ensemble commitment — across the production process.
I can engage with First Peoples theatrical and storytelling traditions with cultural respect and understanding.
I can articulate how theatre functions as a tool for cultural transmission, community building, and social change.

areas.name: Advanced Acting Technique
areas.raw: Advanced acting methods, character development, and performance skills.
topics: Stanislavski's system: given circumstances, objectives, actions, emotional memory, and units
Physical theatre approaches: Lecoq, Viewpoints, and movement-based acting
Brechtian technique: alienation effect, gestus, and politically conscious performance
Vocal development: advanced resonance, range, dialect work, and verse speaking
Auditioning skills: monologue selection, preparation, and cold reading

areas.name: Devised and Applied Theatre
areas.raw: Devised theatre, applied theatre, and community performance.
topics: Devising methodologies: physical warm-up, text, research, and image-based creation
Applied theatre forms: drama in education, community theatre, forum theatre, and theatre for social change
Documentary and verbatim theatre: creating from interviews and primary sources
Interdisciplinary performance: combining dance, music, and visual elements
Developing original scripts for community and social contexts

areas.name: Script Analysis and Directing
areas.raw: Script analysis, dramaturgy, and introduction to directing.
topics: Advanced script analysis: sub-text, dramatic structure, and dramaturgical questions
Production concept development: director's vision and design collaboration
Blocking principles: staging for meaning, variety, and visual storytelling
Introduction to directing: working with actors, giving notes, and production planning
Stage management fundamentals: prompt book, cue sheets, and production schedule

areas.name: Theatre Traditions and Contemporary Practice
areas.raw: Diverse theatrical traditions, First Peoples performance, and contemporary theatre.
topics: First Peoples performance traditions: contemporary Indigenous theatre — Tomson Highway, Drew Hayden Taylor
Contemporary Canadian theatre: significant playwrights and regional theatre contexts
World theatre traditions: African, Asian, Latin American, and diaspora theatre
Theatre and social justice: feminist theatre, queer theatre, and postcolonial drama
Theatre criticism: reading and writing analytical reviews
